school.title: IEEE CIS Summer School 2026
school.subtitle: Computational Intelligence in Robotics
school.description: Join us for an intensive four-day summer school focusing on the latest advancements in Computational Intelligence applied to Robotics. This program features world-renowned speakers, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities for PhD students and early-career researchers.

sections.title: About the Summer School
sections.content: Foundation models are fundamentally transforming artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics. Integrating these models into robotics and embodied AI marks a decisive paradigm shift from rigid, task-specific pipelines to scalable, data-driven intelligence capable of generalization and adaptation in the real world. This summer school explores the role of foundation and generative models in robotic planning, control, perception, and design, with the goal of developing adaptable, context-aware autonomous robots. The topics covered in this summer school are:
points: Generative models for robotic control and planning

items.topic: Can we build drones that not only think but also adapt?
items.abstract: An abstract of the talk will be available soon.

sections.title: Venue & Travel
sections.content: Paderborn is a historic city in Germany. The summer school will be held at the University of Paderborn.

sections.title: Social Activities
sections.content: Beyond the technical sessions, we have organized several social events to help you network and enjoy the beautiful city of Paderborn.

socialItems.title: Welcome Dinner
socialItems.description: A welcome dinner to facilitate initial introductions among participants in a relaxed atmosphere.

socialItems.title: Guided City Tour
socialItems.description: A guided city tour organized by the City of Paderborn, showcasing the rich history and landmarks of the city.

socialItems.title: Outdoor Archery
socialItems.description: An outdoor archery activity designed to promote team spirit, focus, and precision among attendees.

socialItems.title: MuseumsForum Visit
socialItems.description: A farewell visit to the Heinz Nixdorf MuseumsForum, the world’s largest computer museum, exploring the history of computing.

sections.title: Registration
sections.content: Registration is open to graduate students and researchers. IEEE CIS members receive a discounted rate. Applications must include a CV and a short statement of interest.
